title: First microphone at 5 Palmas
description: The neighborhood as stage, truth as the starting point.
longDescription: In La Lisa, two voices find rap as their own language. It is not spectacle: it is necessity. From this origin La Aldea is born — a community that would grow around the duo and those who recognized themselves in their lyrics.

title: The duo takes shape
description: Al2 and El B seal a single identity: Los Aldeanos.
longDescription: Aldo Roberto Rodríguez Baquero (Al2) and Bian Oscar Rodríguez Galá (El B) unite metaphor, street, and philosophy. The underground stops being a hobby and becomes a shared life project.

title: Censurados
description: A record that redefines what Cuban rap can say.
longDescription: With Censurados, the duo takes conscious rap to another scale. Social critique, sharp poetry, and songs that would speak to entire generations — including those not yet born.

title: Poesía Esposada
description: Lyrical maturity: love, protest, and freedom on one record.
longDescription: The duo proves sensitivity does not weaken the message. Poesía Esposada is a bridge between the neighborhood and deep reflection — a record to listen calmly and reread urgently.

title: El Atropello
longDescription: At their most expansive moment, Los Aldeanos carry the message to more listeners without softening the truth. The record confirms the duo's legacy is also a legacy of intellectual courage.

title: Closing of the joint era
description: End of the duo, beginning of collective memory.
longDescription: Los Aldeanos close their chapter together, but what they built remains: songs, phrases, critical attitude. The aldeana community does not end — it becomes archive, memory, and pride.

title: Appendix: solo paths
description: Brief note at the margin of the main archive.
longDescription: Al2 and El B continue separately. This site documents above all the decade they were one, because that is where the heart of the shared legacy lives.
